summarization.a: The figure shows the growth rate (Å/cycle) as a function of the deposition temperature (°C). The growth rate of the material decreases as the deposition temperature increases, reaching at minimum at around 320 °C before slightly increasing again.

a.question: What is described in the figure?
a.answer: The figure shows the growth per cycles (Å/cycle) for different temperatures of deposition (between 250 °C and 350 °C).

a.question: What effect does increasing the deposition temperature have on the growth rate?
a.answer: As the deposition temperature increases, the growth per cycle decreases up to a temperature of 25 °C. After this point, growth per cycle increases again.

a.question: Which temperature leads to the lowest growth rate?
a.answer: 325 °C leads to the lowest growth rate.

a.question: What advantage is shown when the deposition temperature is 250 °C?
a.answer: At this point, growth per cycle reaches its maximum value, enabling deeper etching in a single cycle.
